chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ دُعَاءٌ مُوَقَّتٌ وَ أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ فِيهَا تَسْلِيمٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 55 – There is no Prescribed supplication in the Salaat and there is no greeting in it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/64)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/64)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لَيْسَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ عَلَى الْمَيِّتِ تَسْلِيمٌ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Abdullah Bin Muskan, from Al Halby who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws said, ‘There is no greeting in the Salaat upon the deceased’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn Sinan from ’Abd Allah ibn Muskan from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘There is no Salam (the phrase of offering greeting of peace) to end Salah (prayer) for a deceased.’”
